posts.text:
Old lady groyper
Measuring skulls with a tape measure makes her annoyed
Elderly foid
If you leave the infrastructure of surveillance in place, people in power will inevitably find an excuse to resume using it. You have to take that shit down.
I have an extremely long and highly googleable track record of challenging power so idk who you’re talking about
Just incredible. The aloha of setting everything you claimed to believe on fire to preserve your access to power, a power that delights in humiliating you.

"According to the American Automobile Association, the national average for a gallon of regular gasoline jumped to $3.79 on Tuesday, up from $2.98 before the U.S. and Israel launched the war against Iran."
apnews.com/live/iran-wa...
They don't build prisons to keep them empty.
Gonna need to remember to buy the merch this time

No. There’s a sneer, an irony, a bite to “ethics-free” that “corrupt” can’t capture. Writing is not reducing fractions. Writing is wringing the capacious meaning out of words through effort. Sometimes that effort takes longer.

Chris is mad at Wired not being part of the “techno-optimism” future? It should be fucking obvious by now that that was cover for the rise of a dystopic tech oligarchy. Chris wanted his place in the sun under it.

I love seeing Chris Anderson criticize Katie. Chris was so ethics-free that he put HIS OWN DRONE COMPANY, WHICH HE OWNED WHILE EDITING WIRED, at the heart of a cover package. I was covering drones as death-instruments for Wired at the time & wasn’t even told about the cover. Wont happen under Katie!
Tulsi Gabbard. Trapped in a cage of her own beclowning. The most cucked person in the Trump administration (if we’re excluding the spouses of Noem and Lewandowski)
I wish I could tell my 2006 self that in 20 years there's going to be a drone strike on the top floors of the al-Rasheed hotel in the Green Zone.

Two weeks into the war, this is the bottom line: The Strait of Hormuz has *become* the objective. Trump has lost control, to the point where the closure of the Strait & its global economic implications have overtaken all the boastful goals of regime change or destroying the Iranian missile arsenal
It ain’t reached its final form yet, is what I mean
So, up to us to prevent this future.

We will likely retain vestiges of institutions of representation to provide a false sense of continuity. Symbols of nations will remain, to draw energies away from class-based allegiances, while actual governance occurs through corporations and ratified through empty electoralism
The future is subscription-tiered citizenship, where your access to resources and the freedoms they provide is throttled and woe to those who can’t afford them.
Afraid I would run out of characters.

Meta is dropping encrypted messages from Instagram DMs from 8 May. 

This means that your all private conversations will be readable by Meta, shareable with advertisers, and subpoenable by courts.

Privacy should be a default. Sign our petition.
www.mozillafoundation.org/petitions/ke...

None of These People I Insulted Want To Die For Me in The Strait of Hormuz?. The Trump people, on top of everything else, do not know ball. PLUS: U.S. military AI usage killed an Iraqi student in 2024
The Decline and Fall of American Sea Power (ft/American Prestige). Hard truths about naval sustainment and the Iran War. In podcast form

ICE Tried To Turn This Minneapolis Teacher Into An Informant. Brandon Siguenza was detained for doing legal-observer work. ICE's real interest was in generating snitches. PLUS: Mojtaba Khamanei isn't surrendering

The Water Wars Are Here, And They're Not Woke. The Iran War's latest horrifying component involves attacks on desalination facilities. A "not politically correct" conflict could render much of the region uninhabitable

When The War Goes AI, The Data Centers Will Be Targets (f/Engadget's Devindra Hardawar). We talk Anthropic, OpenAI, Iran and the mil-tech oligarchy. Children will be sent to protect data centers the way their fathers were sent to protect oil fields
Bibi's Iran Goals Are Not Necessarily Trump's Iran Goals (Director's Cut)
Interceptors Versus Missiles: The Iran War's Wrath of The Math. A theory of Iranian strategy. PLUS: Me, Chris Hayes and Ayman Mohyeldin on the war's politics
Abolition Vs. Empire, At Home As It Is Abroad. A new essay of mine for The Nation. It predicted the Iran War. I had to rewrite a bunch of it over the weekend. Stay for the part about abolishing JSOC
Regime Change in Iran (Terms And Conditions Apply). The warmongers, once again, get the war they've sought for decades. Trump and Netanyahu leave the hard work to the people they're bombing
America Doesn’t Make Oppenheimers Like We Used To. What business did Dario Amodei think he was getting into when Anthropic signed on as a Pentagon contractor?
The Scent of Iranian Lavender. The U.S. and Israel are gearing up for war with Iran while the Pentagon demands an AI company drop its "safeguards." Hmm. But yes: abuse the Defense Production Act!

Dan Caine Suddenly Realizes He's The Iran War's Fall Guy. The white DEI hire at the Joint Chiefs of Staff might want to deny calling an Iran war "easy." But his job is to insulate Trump from blame for military debacles
The Meaning of Marco Rubio's Munich Speech Is Found in The 'Board of Peace'. A new institution is being born. In Munich, Marco Rubio outlined a certain purpose. When will the two join up?
The War on Terror in Minnesota (ft. Jordan Uhl & The Intercept). A lot of you have been asking me for this. Here it is, in podcast form
Ron Wyden Only Talks Like This When The Spies Do Something *Real* Bad. No, I don't know what they did. But I have a lot of experience with the senator
